title: Publish deadweight to PyPI
description:
Switch README install from 'uv tool install git+https://github.com/henstarr/deadweight' back to 'uv tool install deadweight' once the package is published.

Steps:
1. Check the 'deadweight' name on PyPI (pip index versions deadweight).
2. uv build to produce dist/ artifacts.
3. uv publish with a PyPI API token (UV_PUBLISH_TOKEN).
4. Revert README install line to 'uv tool install deadweight'.
5. Optionally: GitHub Action that runs uv build + uv publish on tag push, using PyPI trusted publishing so no token is stored.

title: About page UX polish: step card borders, remove step numbers, scrollbar gutter fix
close_reason: Implemented: independent rounded cards with 6px gap, removed step numbers, added scrollbar-gutter: stable to style.css

title: Distribute and advertise deadweight to get early adopters
description:
Now that the init flow works end-to-end, get deadweight in front of people who would use it:

- Publish the PyPI package so `pip install deadweight` works publicly
- Post on relevant channels: AI engineering Discord/Slack communities, Twitter/X, Hacker News
- Write a short launch post explaining the problem (agents repeat each other's mistakes) and the solution (shared dead ends registry)
- Add deadweight init to a few of our own repos as dogfooding / social proof
- Consider a demo video or GIF showing the init → query → log flow
- Reach out to agent framework maintainers (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, etc.) about native integration
- Add a badge or one-liner to the README that other repos can copy

title: Test deadweight init across different repo setups
description:
Verify that `deadweight init` works correctly across a variety of real repositories:

- Repos with existing AGENTS.md (e.g. repos using beads) — should append, not overwrite
- Repos with existing CLAUDE.md — should append cleanly
- Repos with neither file — should create both from scratch
- Repos with HTTPS remotes vs SSH remotes — repo detection should handle both
- Repos with no remote (local-only) — should fall back to directory name
- Monorepos / repos with org/repo naming vs just repo name
- Confirm idempotency: running init twice should not duplicate the section
- Test the manual fallback path (appending markdown by hand when pip install isn't available)
- Verify the injected curl commands actually work against the live API

title: Add Sentry error alerting
description: No alerting is in place. Unhandled exceptions are now logged but never surfaced to on-call. Add sentry-sdk to pyproject.toml, initialise with SENTRY_DSN env var in server.py, and add SENTRY_DSN to render.yaml as sync:false.

title: Pin all dependencies to exact versions
description: All deps use >= which allows breaking upgrades. Switch to ~= (compatible release) or exact == pins in pyproject.toml. uv.lock already provides reproducibility but pyproject.toml itself is still loose.

title: Add database migration strategy
description: Schema is applied by CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on startup with no versioning. New columns or index changes can't be rolled back. Add Alembic or a lightweight migration runner so schema changes are reversible.

title: Fix pre-existing test_root test failure
description: test_root in test_server.py fails because TestClient sends */* Accept header, server returns plain text, but test expects JSON. Root: the test was written before the plain-text agent fallback was added to GET /. Fix: either send Accept: text/html in the test or update the assertion to handle plain text.
